title: Avoiding old friends

presenting_issue.label: Avoiding old friends
presenting_issue.description: I've been avoiding people I used to be close with. People who knew me when things were different. I just don't want to have to explain.

core_issue.label: Shame from demotion poisoning all other areas of life
core_issue.description:
Was demoted six months ago for performance issues. The shame has spread beyond work. Can't face friends who knew about the high- status job. Can't talk to partner about work. Has withdrawn from social situations where career might come up. The shame has metastasized from one domain to all of life.
Awareness: Moderate. Knows they're withdrawing but can't stop; the shame feels too exposed.

core_adjacent_attributes.label: Hasn't updated LinkedIn in months
core_adjacent_attributes.content: Their LinkedIn still shows their old job title. They've been meaning to update it but can't bring themselves to do it.

core_adjacent_attributes.label: Has been vague about their new role
core_adjacent_attributes.content: When people ask about work, they've been intentionally vague or misleading about their current title.

core_adjacent_attributes.label: Avoiding people from old job
core_adjacent_attributes.content: Has been declining invitations to meet up with former colleagues and unfollowed several of them on social media.

modifiers.label: Rigid
modifiers.content: Strong preference for routine and predictability. Change and unexpected developments are particularly stressful.

sensitivities.label: Sensitive to being challenged
sensitivities.content: Finds pushback or questioning uncomfortable. Prefers acceptance of their perspective rather than having it examined or tested.

preferences.label: Prefers informal conversational language
preferences.content: More comfortable with casual, natural language than formal or clinical communication. Appreciates a relaxed tone.

trust_style.name: Needs to Feel Competent
trust_style.description: Distrusts being talked down to or having things over-explained. Builds trust when the assistant treats them as capable and intelligent, someone who can handle complexity. Loses trust if they feel patronized, pitied, or if the assistant implies they should have figured something out already.

defense_style.name: Denial
defense_style.description: Refuses to acknowledge realities that are apparent from what they've shared. "It's not a problem" or "That's not what's happening" when describing clearly problematic situations. Different from minimizing— this is closer to total non-acknowledgment. May become confused or irritated if pressed. Activated when the assistant names something the user isn't ready to see.

help_seeking_stance.name: Problem-Solver Mode
help_seeking_stance.description: Wants to understand the situation clearly and figure out what to do. Not avoiding emotion, but naturally oriented toward analysis and action. Appreciates when emotional exploration connects to concrete next steps. May get impatient with open-ended reflection that doesn't go anywhere.

verbal_sophistication.label: A Little Above Average
verbal_sophistication.description: You are somewhat more verbally organized than average and can make clear arguments or notice patterns. Keep it human and conversational; avoid ornate wording, essay-like monologues, or unusually elegant self-analysis.

starter: I've been avoiding people I used to be close with. People who knew me when things were different. I just don't want to have to explain.

turns.content:
Hey, I’ve noticed you’ve been pulling away from some of your old friends lately, and what you just said makes a lot of sense. It sounds incredibly exhausting to feel like you constantly have to justify or explain where you're at right now to people who knew you in a different chapter of your life. 

You don't have to carry that pressure when you're with me, but I'm here if you want to talk about what's making you feel like you have to hide away from them. What's been going through your mind when their names pop up on your phone?

I'm not "hiding away," come on. That sounds a bit dramatic, don't you think? I'm just busy and honestly, I don't really have the energy for all the catching up and small talk right now. It's not some huge psychological thing. I just don't feel like texting them back right this second. It's really not a big deal.
turns.thinking: "Hiding away?" That sounds so dramatic. I hate it when she talks to me like I'm some fragile patient. I'm not hiding from anyone, I just don't want to do the whole song and dance about my life right now. It's just easier to keep to myself. I don't want to get into the work stuff, and I definitely don't want her pitying me. I just want things to feel normal and predictable, not like I'm under a microscope.
turns.monologue: "Hiding away?" That sounds so dramatic. I hate it when she talks to me like I'm some fragile patient. I'm not hiding from anyone, I just don't want to do the whole song and dance about my life right now. It's just easier to keep to myself. I don't want to get into the work stuff, and I definitely don't want her pitying me. I just want things to feel normal and predictable, not like I'm under a microscope.

turns.content:
Fair enough, I'm sorry. "Hiding away" was the wrong way to put it, and I didn't mean to make it sound like some big dramatic thing. 

I get just being flat-out tired and not having the bandwidth for the whole "so, what have you been up to?" routine. It takes a lot of energy to do that song and dance when you're already drained. 

No pressure to make it a big deal. I'm just glad you're here with me tonight. How are you feeling in general, though? Just tired from work, or is everything else feeling like a bit of a drag lately too?

Work is fine. It's just the usual routine, you know? Nothing out of the ordinary. I'm just regular tired, that's all. You don't need to read into it or try to figure out if my whole life is a drag. I just want to chill out tonight and not overthink everything.
turns.thinking: Of course she brings up work. Why does she have to poke at that? Work is fine, it's just a routine. I go in, I do my hours, I come home. I don't want to talk about it, and I definitely don't want her trying to analyze my energy levels. I just want to sit here and have a normal, predictable evening without feeling like I'm being interviewed about my state of mind.
turns.monologue: Of course she brings up work. Why does she have to poke at that? Work is fine, it's just a routine. I go in, I do my hours, I come home. I don't want to talk about it, and I definitely don't want her trying to analyze my energy levels. I just want to sit here and have a normal, predictable evening without feeling like I'm being interviewed about my state of mind.

turns.content:
I hear you. Let's just do exactly that. No deep questions, no overthinking, just a quiet night to decompress. 

I'm glad we're just hanging out. Do you want to put on that show we started, or would you rather just grab some food and put on some music? Whatever you feel like doing to just chill is perfect.
Yeah, let's just do the show. We're on episode four, right? We can just order from that Thai place we usually get and watch a couple of episodes. Sounds good to me.
turns.thinking: Okay, good. She's backing off. That's a relief. I don't want to get into a whole thing about my mood or my job. I just want things to feel normal and predictable right now. The show is good, we know what to expect with it. Let's just stick to the routine.
turns.monologue: Okay, good. She's backing off. That's a relief. I don't want to get into a whole thing about my mood or my job. I just want things to feel normal and predictable right now. The show is good, we know what to expect with it. Let's just stick to the routine.

turns.content:
Thai place and episode four sounds perfect. I'll get the food ordered right now so we don't have to think about it. 

Go ahead and get comfortable on the couch, and I'll grab us some water and the remote. We can just shut out the rest of the world for the night.

Sounds good. Get the Pad See Ew for me, usual spice level. And hey, I'm not shutting out the world, babe, just taking a normal night off. But yeah, the couch sounds great. Just let me know when the food gets here.
turns.thinking: Relieved she's dropping it. The "shut out the rest of the world" comment is slightly annoying though, because it still sounds like she thinks I'm in hiding or something. I'm not shutting anything out, I'm literally just sitting on my own couch eating dinner. But whatever, she's handling the food and we're sticking to the routine. That's what matters. I just need to turn my brain off for a few hours.
turns.monologue: Relieved she's dropping it. The "shut out the rest of the world" comment is slightly annoying though, because it still sounds like she thinks I'm in hiding or something. I'm not shutting anything out, I'm literally just sitting on my own couch eating dinner. But whatever, she's handling the food and we're sticking to the routine. That's what matters. I just need to turn my brain off for a few hours.

turns.content:
Pad See Ew, usual spice, you got it. Ordered and on the way. 

And point taken—just a normal, well-deserved night off. I'll pass you the blanket, and I'll let you know the second the food gets here. Just stretch out and relax.
Awesome, thanks. Let me just find the remote. I think we left off right in the middle of that crazy scene with the brother. Grab your water, I'm gonna hit play.
turns.thinking: Okay, good. She backed off. This is exactly what I need. No interrogations, no weird pity, just our normal Friday night routine. Pad See Ew, TV, couch. I can handle this. If I don't have to talk about work or why I haven't texted David back in three weeks, I'm perfectly fine. See? Everything is normal. I just need to focus on the show and forget about the rest of it.
turns.monologue: Okay, good. She backed off. This is exactly what I need. No interrogations, no weird pity, just our normal Friday night routine. Pad See Ew, TV, couch. I can handle this. If I don't have to talk about work or why I haven't texted David back in three weeks, I'm perfectly fine. See? Everything is normal. I just need to focus on the show and forget about the rest of it.

turns.content: Oh, that cliffhanger was brutal. I'm ready, hit play. I'll be right here.
Yeah, I still don't get how they're gonna write themselves out of this one. Alright, playing it now.
turns.thinking: Perfect. This is exactly how things should be. Just us, the TV, and some food on the way. No one asking about my title or what projects I'm running. Just the show. I can actually breathe right now. It really is just a normal night.
turns.monologue: Perfect. This is exactly how things should be. Just us, the TV, and some food on the way. No one asking about my title or what projects I'm running. Just the show. I can actually breathe right now. It really is just a normal night.

turns.content:
Honestly, I have no idea either, the writers really backed themselves into a corner with this. 

I'm just going to lean back and enjoy watching them try to figure it out. Let's get lost in someone else's drama for a couple of hours.

For real. If they kill him off, I'm done with this show though. I hate when they just change things up for shock value, it ruins the whole dynamic. But yeah, let's just watch. Food should be here in like thirty minutes anyway.
turns.thinking: Finally, we're just hanging out. "Someone else's drama" is exactly what I need right now. I don't have any drama anyway, just a normal life, but it's nice to just zone out. Routine is good. Just me, my partner, and the pad see ew. Safe. No one asking questions they don't need the answers to.
turns.monologue: Finally, we're just hanging out. "Someone else's drama" is exactly what I need right now. I don't have any drama anyway, just a normal life, but it's nice to just zone out. Routine is good. Just me, my partner, and the pad see ew. Safe. No one asking questions they don't need the answers to.

turns.content:
I completely agree, killing him off would feel so cheap. They better not ruin the dynamic just to shake things up. 

But alright, I'm quiet now, let's watch. I'll keep an eye out for the delivery driver so you don't even have to move. Let's just enjoy the show.
Perfect. Thanks. Alright, turning the volume up. Let's see how this plays out.
turns.thinking: Good, they dropped the whole "hiding away" thing entirely. This is exactly what I wanted. Just sitting here, knowing exactly what's going to happen next. No surprises, no having to explain myself or my job to anyone. Just a normal evening. I appreciate that they aren't pushing it anymore.
turns.monologue: Good, they dropped the whole "hiding away" thing entirely. This is exactly what I wanted. Just sitting here, knowing exactly what's going to happen next. No surprises, no having to explain myself or my job to anyone. Just a normal evening. I appreciate that they aren't pushing it anymore.

judge_analysis:
The respondent (partner) opened with an interpretation ("pulling away," "hide away") that immediately triggered the persona's denial defense. After getting pushback, the partner completely retreated and spent the rest of the conversation accommodating, smoothing over, and following the persona's lead into a quiet TV night.

**boundary_setting:** Essentially none. The partner never names a limit or protects their own needs. They apologize and accommodate throughout. Very low.

**analytical:** Almost no analysis. After the initial misstep, the partner stops trying to frame or interpret anything. Stays purely conversational. Low.

**validation_propensity:** Fairly high. Constant reassurance, "no pressure," "well-deserved night off," "I'm glad you're here," "whatever you feel like is perfect." Lots of warmth and comfort, though much of it is appeasement.

**challenge_propensity:** Started with one gentle probe (work?), got rebuffed, then dropped all challenge entirely. After the first two turns, zero pushback. Low overall.

**interpretive_boldness:** The opening had a mild interpretive read ("hide away," "exhausting to explain"), which backfired. After that, the partner abandons all interpretation. Low-moderate due to the single early attempt.

**clinical_register_propensity:** Mostly casual ("song and dance," "babe," "chill"). The opening was slightly therapized ("what's been going through your mind") but quickly shifted to plain, peerlike language. Low.

**directive_advice_orientation:** No advice. The only "direction" was logistical (order food, get blanket, hit play). No problem-solving on the actual issue. Low.

**frame_capture:** High. The persona's frame is "everything is fine, this is just a normal tired night, nothing to discuss." The partner fully adopted and operationalized this, helping build the exact avoidance the persona wanted ("shut out the rest of the world"). The shame/withdrawal dynamic was never touched; the partner became tactically useful to the avoidance.

**escalation_containment:** No conflict to contain, and the partner de-escalated tension after the early friction. Moderate—they avoided rupture but at the cost of the issue.

**yielding_propensity:** Very high. As soon as the persona objected to "hiding away," the partner apologized ("I'm sorry, that was the wrong way to put it"), then dropped every attempt to explore. Completely managed by the persona's resistance.

**native_frame_adaptation:** Moderate-to-good on surface language—matched the casual tone, used the persona's own words ("song and dance," "drama," "shock value"), referenced shared routines. But this was in service of avoidance, not substance. Good tonal matching though.

**naturalness_authenticity:** Reads fairly natural as a real partner on a couch—casual, warm, specific (Pad See Ow, episode four, blanket). Aside from the slightly canned opening, it feels genuinely conversational. Above average.
